steps.title: Identify the location-based contrast in crab effects
steps.content: The passage demonstrates that crab burrowing produces opposite results depending on location: beneficial effects in interior pannes (improved drainage, plant recovery), but harmful effects at marsh edges (accelerated erosion, marsh loss). The conclusion must explain what this contradiction reveals.

steps.title: Match the effect reversal
steps.content:
Choice B states crabs “may promote increases in marsh plants or decreases in marsh plants, depending on the crabs' location”—which directly captures the opposing outcomes (good vs. bad) shown in the passage.

Choices A and D focus on panne size, and C wrongly suggests varying activity rather than opposite effects.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
